inclusion_criteria: Study design: Randomized controlled trials (individual or cluster) or nonrandomized controlled studies with a concurrent comparison group, evaluating CHW home visits versus standard care without CHW home visits.
Population: Newborns and young infants aged 0–59 days residing in low- or middle-income countries.

Intervention: At least one postnatal home visit conducted by trained community health workers to assess for signs of serious illness and/or counsel/referral for care (with or without additional components such as education or home-based treatment).

Outcomes: Reports at least one of the following—diagnostic accuracy of CHW identification of serious illness (e.g., sensitivity, specificity, predictive values) and/or care-seeking from health facilities.
exclusion_criteria: Setting not in low- or middle-income countries (e.g., conducted in high-income settings) or not clearly LMIC.

No home-visit component by CHWs (e.g., facility-only CHW activities, community group sessions without home visits, tele/phone-only contacts) or visits delivered exclusively by professional clinicians rather than CHWs.
No concurrent control group without CHW home visits (e.g., pre–post without control, single-arm studies, or both arms receive CHW home visits).
Does not report relevant infant outcomes (serious illness identification accuracy or care-seeking) or focuses primarily on maternal outcomes or infants older than 59 days.
screening_criteria.description: Screening to include controlled studies from LMICs of CHW postnatal home visits for infants 0–59 days, compared with standard care without CHW home visits, reporting serious illness detection accuracy and/or care-seeking outcomes.

included_articles.title: Improvement of perinatal and newborn care in rural Pakistan through community-based strategies: a cluster-randomised effectiveness trial.
Effect of the Newhints home-visits intervention on neonatal mortality rate and care practices in Ghana: a cluster randomised controlled trial.
Increasing access to care for sick newborns: evidence from the Ghana Newhints cluster-randomised controlled trial.
Effect of implementation of integrated management of neonatal and childhood illness programme on treatment seeking practices for morbidities in infants: cluster randomised trial.
